# Booking SvelteKit Template Spec

Status: ready local baseline
Template ID: `booking-sveltekit`
Version: `0.1.0`
Primary runtime: SvelteKit on Cloudflare Workers
Scaffold path: `templates/booking-sveltekit`

## Decision

Create a full Cloudflare SvelteKit booking app as the first complete user-facing template after the core module/update workflow is stable. The local-baseline template now exists under `templates/booking-sveltekit`; keep evolving it against this spec.

This should become the default demo template because it proves more than an API scaffold:

- visible customer booking flow
- admin booking management
- D1-backed business data
- module docs and lockfile
- upgrade planning
- safe hooks for business-specific customization
- managed Cloudflare preview path

Keep the current Hono generator as `booking-api-hono` or an internal API-only template.

The template starts in this repo at `templates/booking-sveltekit`. Keep it here while contracts are changing; split templates into a dedicated repository or packages only after the official template set and release process are stable.

## API Logic Boundary

The template must detach API/domain logic from SvelteKit routes.

SvelteKit route handlers should:

- parse request input
- call a detached use case
- map framework-neutral results to SvelteKit responses

They should not own:

- booking availability calculations
- booking status transitions
- hold expiration behavior
- payment confirmation behavior
- email/reminder side effects
- audit/domain event semantics

Detached use cases live in the prebuilt module package `modules/booking` and are consumed through `@microservices-sh/booking`. Dependency ports live under `modules/booking/src/ports`, and concrete adapters live under `modules/booking/src/adapters`.

## Target User

AI-heavy dev agencies, consultants, fractional CTOs, and technical founders who need to launch or customize a bookable service business quickly.

Examples:

- clinic appointment intake
- studio class/session booking
- consultant discovery calls
- local service appointments
- paid workshops or events

## Runtime

Use SvelteKit with `@sveltejs/adapter-cloudflare`, built for Cloudflare Workers with static assets.

Cloudflare baseline:

- Worker app generated by SvelteKit build
- D1 binding: `DB`
- KV binding: `CACHE_KV`
- optional Queue binding: `NOTIFICATIONS`
- optional R2 binding: `FILES`
- optional Turnstile env/binding for public forms
- `nodejs_compat` only when a dependency requires it

The template must access Cloudflare bindings through SvelteKit server code, not client code.

## Product Scope

### Included In MVP

- homepage with booking entry point
- service selection
- availability lookup
- customer details form
- booking confirmation
- booking lookup page
- simple admin dashboard
- admin booking list/detail
- admin customer list/detail
- admin membership tiers and customer credit ledgers
- basic settings config
- audit and domain event recording
- generated module docs
- generated project CLI
- upgrade-plan support

### Deferred

- staff calendar sync
- multi-location scheduling
- recurring bookings
- subscriptions
- full CRM pipeline
- visual workflow builder
- drag-and-drop page editor
- full marketplace install flow
- production-grade auth provider integration

## Modules

### Required

| Slot | Module | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| `identity` | `auth` | Sessions, roles, admin/member separation |
| `profile` | `customer` | Customer records, contact fields, consent |
| `scheduling` | `booking` | Services, availability, bookings, cancellation rules |
| `membershipCredits` | `membership-credits` | Customer memberships, tiers, credit balances, and credit ledger |
| `audit` | `audit-log` | Mutation trail and domain events |

### Optional

| Slot | Module | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| `payment` | `payment-stripe` | Checkout, deposits, refunds, webhooks |
| `messaging` | `email` | Confirmation and reminder emails |
| `admin` | `admin-shell` | Richer admin UI and RBAC screens |
| `analytics` | `analytics` | Funnel and operational metrics |

Optional modules must begin as `--plan` only if they introduce secrets, webhooks, migrations, queues, or remote side effects.

## Route Map

### Public Routes

| Route | Purpose | Module Slots |
|-------|---------|--------------|
| `/` | Public booking landing screen | booking, customer |
| `/book` | service/date/time/customer form | booking, customer |
| `/book/confirm` | confirmation result | booking, customer, optional payment/email |
| `/booking/[id]` | customer booking lookup | booking, customer |
| `/account` | customer account shell | auth, customer |

### Admin Routes

| Route | Purpose | Module Slots |
|-------|---------|--------------|
| `/admin` | operational dashboard | auth, booking, customer, audit |
| `/admin/bookings` | booking list | booking |
| `/admin/bookings/[id]` | booking detail and status actions | booking, audit |
| `/admin/customers` | customer list | customer |
| `/admin/customers/[id]` | customer detail | customer, booking |
| `/admin/settings` | template and module config view | all |

### Server Endpoints

| Endpoint | Purpose | Gate |
|----------|---------|------|
| `GET /api/availability` | compute available booking slots | normal |
| `POST /api/customers` | create or update customer during booking | PII |
| `POST /api/bookings` | create booking | PII, mutation |
| `POST /api/bookings/[id]/cancel` | cancel booking | mutation |
| `GET /api/admin/bookings` | admin list | auth/admin |
| `GET /api/admin/customers` | admin list | auth/admin |
| `POST /api/webhooks/stripe` | payment webhook | optional, high risk |

## Data Model

Core D1 tables:

- `users`
- `sessions`
- `customers`
- `service_types`
- `availability_rules`
- `blackout_windows`
- `bookings`
- `booking_status_history`
- `domain_events`
- `audit_events`

Optional module tables:

- `payments`
- `payment_events`
- `email_templates`
- `email_deliveries`

Schema ownership:

- module-owned tables are documented in module docs
- template-owned seed data lives in `migrations/template`
- optional provider migrations require approval
- remote D1 migrations require explicit production approval

## Project Structure

```txt
.
  microservices.template.json
  microservices.config.json
  microservices.lock.json
  README.agent.md
  docs/
    llms.txt
    modules/
    templates/booking-sveltekit.md
  migrations/
    0001_core.sql
    0002_booking_seed.sql
  src/
    app.d.ts
    hooks.server.ts
    routes/
      +layout.svelte
      +page.svelte
      book/+page.svelte
      book/+page.server.ts
      book/confirm/+page.svelte
      booking/[id]/+page.server.ts
      admin/+layout.server.ts
      admin/+page.svelte
      admin/bookings/+page.server.ts
      api/availability/+server.ts
      api/bookings/+server.ts
    lib/
      components/
        booking/
        admin/
        ui/
      config/
        template.ts
      server/
        adapters/
          sveltekit-response.ts
        hooks/
          booking.ts
          customer.ts
          notification.ts
        db/
          client.ts
          schema.ts
        events/
          emit.ts
        permissions/
          require-role.ts
  scripts/
    microservices.js
  tests/
  wrangler.jsonc
modules/
  booking/
    module.json
    src/
      index.ts
      use-cases/
      ports/
      adapters/
      schemas.ts
      hooks.ts
    migrations/
```

## Customization Slots

### Config

`microservices.config.json` should expose:

- business name
- timezone
- currency
- service types
- default duration
- slot interval
- lead time
- cancellation window
- customer fields
- consent fields
- theme tokens
- admin role names

### Hooks

Hook files should be stable and documented:

- `beforeCustomerCreate`
- `afterCustomerUpdated`
- `calculateAvailability`
- `beforeBookingCreate`
- `afterBookingConfirmed`
- `beforeBookingCancel`
- `renderConfirmationMessage`
- `afterPaymentSucceeded`

### UI Overlays

Allow overlays for:

- homepage hero section
- booking form fields
- service card component
- confirmation page
- admin booking row actions
- admin customer detail panels

Overlay files must be listed in `microservices.lock.json` customizations.

### Provider Slots

Provider slots must be swappable:

- `payment`: none, Stripe, later Lemon Squeezy, Paddle
- `messaging`: none, Cloudflare Email, Resend, Postmark
- `auth`: generated auth, later Clerk/Auth.js/custom
- `analytics`: none, Cloudflare Web Analytics/custom event sink

Provider modules must document secrets, webhooks, events, migrations, and test mode.

## Permission Gates

Require approval before:

- enabling `payment-stripe`
- adding email sends
- changing auth/session logic
- adding webhook endpoints
- changing PII fields
- applying migrations
- creating Cloudflare resources
- deploying preview or production
- modifying forked module internals

Agents may inspect secret names and configured/missing status. They must not request or print secret values.

## Upgrade Behavior

The generated template must support:

```bash
pnpm microservices updates --json
pnpm microservices upgrade booking --plan --json
pnpm microservices upgrade customer --plan --json
pnpm microservices check --json
```

Upgrade plans must classify changes as:

- safe config change
- hook review
- UI overlay merge
- migration review
- provider permission change
- fork/manual merge

The default policy:

- config is preserved
- hooks are preserved and rechecked
- overlays require merge review
- forks are user-owned
- provider upgrades require approval
- production migrations require approval

## LLM Requirements

Generated project docs must include:

- `README.agent.md`
- `docs/llms.txt`
- `docs/modules/catalog.json`
- module Markdown docs
- `docs/templates/booking-sveltekit.md`
- route map
- endpoint payload/response notes
- approval gates
- first safe commands

Agents should be able to answer:

- which modules are installed
- which module owns each route
- which files are safe to edit
- which secrets are needed
- which changes require approval
- how to check upgrades
- how to run local dev

## Acceptance Criteria

The template is ready for implementation when the generated app can:

- install dependencies
- run `pnpm microservices check --json`
- run `pnpm microservices upgrade booking --plan --json`
- apply checked-in D1 migrations locally with `pnpm microservices local migrate`
- start local dev with `pnpm microservices local dev`
- pass HTTP smoke checks with `pnpm microservices local smoke`
- run a Cloudflare deploy dry-run with `pnpm microservices preview deploy --dry-run`
- render public booking flow
- submit a booking
- show admin booking list
- write domain and audit events
- typecheck successfully
- build for Cloudflare

## Why This Is Flexible Enough

The template is flexible because:

- domain logic is module-owned
- app shell is template-owned
- business differences start in config and hooks
- UI differences use overlays
- provider choices use slots
- risky changes are permission-gated
- lockfile snapshots make upgrade diffs explainable
- users own source code and can fork when needed

This gives users a full app on day one without turning the template into a rigid no-code builder or an unmaintainable copied repo.
